During an Interlude XP may be spent to obtain additional 
effects:
 ◆ Recover 2 Soma for each XP spent.
 ◆ Recover 1 Wound for each XP spent.
 ◆ Evolve a Descriptor into a Specialization. For the rest of 
the Scenario, this will grant 1 Additional Success in each 
Scene in which it is used for the first time (1 XP).
 ◆ Obtain a Scenario Gift for the duration of the Scenario 
(1 XP).
Each Interlude, however, represents a use of time. Remember 
that the Scenario, and the NPCs, may continue to act even 
when the Hunters aren’t paying attention.
Rules for the 
Storyteller
Broken Tales is designed to be a modular, easy-to-play role-
playing game. For Storytellers who already have experience 
with role-playing games, all the basic rules are presented 
in this section. In the section Further Details on Game 
Rules (page 17) you will find all the rules explained with 
examples, to clear up doubts about the system and on how to 
handle a game of Broken Tales.
Choose how to play the Scenario
Unlike other RPGs, Broken Tales doesn’t have Scenarios 
with a pre-written plot, but instead cultivates an “emergent” 
narrative. What happens in the Scene will always be the 
outcome of the Hunters’ actions and this, combined with 
the Storyteller’s choices regarding the NPCs, will generate 
situations with unpredictable outcomes. 
An emergent narrative means approaching the game with the 
intention of being surprised by it, reacting on the fly while 
being guided by the rules of the game and t

C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
20
Example: 
Garou the Old Wolf is exploring the area around the town 
of Durfort. Meanwhile, his companions are having a 
conversation with the noble Dubois, hoping to get a grasp 
of what is happening in the Red Hood Iskra Scenario. 
The Storyteller warns Garou of the presence of a rapidly 
approaching pack of wolves. No Check is required because 
it is a piece of information Garou knows (as explained on 
page 16) and in addition, Garou has the Gift Find Them 
Forever After, which allows Garou to perceive noises and 
smells before anyone else. Safely among the foliage, Garou 
prepares an ambush for the new arrivals, determined to make 
immediately clear that this is now his territory. The wolves 
arrive a few meters from the hiding place and Garou decides 
to attack, jumping on them. However, the Storyteller points 
out that wolves have a very acute sense of smell (as reported 
by their Descriptor) and therefore the surprise effect could 
fade before Garou manages to bite one. At this point, the 
Storyteller requires a Defense Check to see if the Hunter will 
really be able to perform the surprise attack. Garou agrees to 
take the risk and does the Check. If not, Garou would have 
remained in hiding waiting for a better opportunity.
Once the situation is clear and defined, we move on to the 
resolution sequence.
The Storyteller:
 ◆ Identifies whether to determine the positive 
Outcome or Failure of an action during the game.
 ◆ Declares the basic Opposition Level of the Thr

C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
101
A Scene Tree consists of a series of Scenes linked 
according to this basic structure:
The First Scene leads to the Second Scene, then there is a 
choice between two options for the Third Scene, Option 
A and Option B. The Fourth Scene leads to a Fifth Scene 
and then there will be either a new fork or the Epilogue, 
depending on the Scenario’s duration. Five Scenes with 
a fork in the middle are optimal for playing a one-shot 
Session of about three hours. Additional forks and Scenes 
will increase playtime by approximately thirty minutes per 
addition.
The First Scene must be structured exactly as we’ve already 
described, but with the addition of a new item: End of 
Scene, which roughly describes which new Scene the 
action will switch to.
The new Scene structure is therefore:
 ◆ A Place where the Scene takes place.
 ◆ A Time, to know when it takes place with respect to 
the previous Scene.
 ◆ An Initial Situation  that defines what happens and 
what to describe to the Hunters before starting the 
Scene.
 ◆ Information, which can be obtained from the Scene. 
Information will act as a “guide” to the Hunters’ choices, 
so it must be explicit. Don’t be afraid to railroad the 
game too much, that’s exactly what we’re looking for!
 ◆ End of Scene , which is a brief description of the 
consequences of the current Scene. The Storyteller will 
use it to direct NPC actions and bring other elements 
to the attention of the Hunters.
Example: The Second Scene of Red-Hood Iskra.

During a game Campaign, a Hunter can use a 
previously acquired Scenario Gift in another 
Scenario if this fits the context and events. 
If this occurs, the Player must bring this 
possibility to the attention of the Storyteller, 
who will decide (evaluating the impact on the 
current Scenario) whether to allow the Hunter 
to use what they have learned in their previous 
adventures. Even in this case, however, the 
limitation remains 1 Scenario Gift per Hunter.
Example: A Scenario Gift from Red-Hood Iskra.
Wolf-killer
Requirement: Having faced the wolves in a fight at least once.
You know how to deal with the fiercest of wolves and are careful 
not to be surrounded. When you obtain at least a Standard 
Outcome on a Position or Defense Check against a pack of 
beasts, you can immediately eliminate 1 Minor animal NPC 
with 1 Wound.
Special Gift: This is a Gift reserved only for the Hunter who 
is the villain of the original fairytale that inspired the Scenario 
being played if present among the Hunters. As soon as the 
requirement is met, the Hunter will automatically gain the 
Gift for the duration of the Scenario, in addition to the ones 
already owned and without having to spend XP . Whether they 
like it or not! The Gift must be built with the rules presented 
in the Hunter’s Creation chapter. Bear in mind that the Gift  
needs to represent a connection between the Hunter and the 
Scenario and not necessarily a mere advantage! When creating 
this Gift, don’t worry as mu

Events of the Scenario
In this Scenario, there are six Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the Hunters put pressure on Carlo:  Lucino will 
intervene to help his friend by giving him time to escape.
If the Hunters reveal their identity to Vincenzo:   
the officer will be at their disposal, trying to help them 
whenever possible. 
If the Hunters meet Geppetto: the man will attempt to 
play on their sympathies with the made-up story of the lost 
son. Geppetto is not a genius, but neither is he stupid. If 
he understands who the Hunters really are, he will try to 
follow them to see if they find Carlo.
If the Hunters gain Carlo’s trust:  the Puppet will 
reveal the truth. Geppetto and Antonio are to blame for 
everything. The Puppet ignores that it is now Azzurra who 
runs things.
If Geppetto is captured by the Hunters:  he will lead 
them to Villa Cilesterino, where his companion is. Geppetto 
will pretend to know nothing, aware that once they arrive, 
Azzurra will get rid of the Hunters.
When Azzurra meets the Hunters:  she will try to 
deceive them by pretending to be a good spirit who tries 
to watch over the inhabitants of Sinalunga. Azzurra will 
ask the Hunters to help her recover a magical Puppet, 
animated by a dark force that is devouring the souls of the 
poor townsfolk.

Wood Scenario and can be acquired once

Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Smile 
in the Darkness Scenario and can be acquired once the 
requirements are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario 
Gift and if they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo 
the previous one.
Voice of the Spirits
Requirement: You have been in contact with either the 
Spirit or Spiritualist Gifts.
With the right peace and focus, you can open your mind 
to the spirit world and communicate with restless souls. 
Spend 1 Soma to contact any creature with the Spirit Gift  
that you’ve previously encountered. Y ou can communicate 
with the Spirit and understand what it says.
Exorcist
Requirement: Have a Church or Magic-related Descriptor. 
Talk to Adrien or Alizée about practices used against Spirits.
Y ou have learned how to use your skills against the Spirits. 
All your actions can affect a target who has the Spirit Gift  
and all Wounds you inflict cannot be ignored by the Spirit 
Gift.
Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are eight Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the Hunters encounter Olivier in the catacombs 
before night falls:  Yvonne will arrive before the 
scoundrel opens his mouth to try and silence him by taking 
her revenge.
If the Hunters talk to more than two poor people 
in the catacombs: Yvonne will notice their presence and 
will come to spy on them, trying to stay hidden.
If Olivier is not found before sunset:

If the Hunters try to leave the Basement: a desperate 
Hatta Carter will attack them with the help of his Automata

If the Hunters corner Alice Liddell:  the Director will

Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the 
Wonderbedlam Scenario and can be acquired once the 
conditions are met. A Hunter can only acquire one Scenario 
Gift. If they buy a new one during an Interlude, they will 
have to forgo the previous one.
Friend of the Rabbits
Requirement: To have captured at least one White Rabbit.
Y ou are able to approach the little white rabbits without 
frightening them. Receive 2 Additional Successes when you 
face the Threat The White Rabbit. Once picked up, your 
new little friends will be able to deliver a message for you to 
any room in Wonder. This Gift allows you to send a White 
Rabbit even through seemingly closed doors.
Lucid Madness
Requirement: Having suffered the Gift Place of Pure 
Madness at least once.
Y ou are slowly figuring out how to make the weirdest ideas 
real. Y ou can spend 1 Soma to try to activate the mad magic 
of Wonder. State something crazy that you are capable of, 
such as I want to walk on walls or I want my arm to stretch 
out of proportion. The Storyteller will ask you for a Position 
Check against an OL proportional to the madness of your 
request.

world. Skutt wants to save Tuvstarr, but without endangering 
her. He cannot enter the pond himself, nor can any other 
inhabitant of these woods.
The magic of the pond binds anyone seeking it to make the 
same journey as the Princess, crossing the places where she 
exchanged her treasures to continue forward. If the Hunters 
bring those treasures to him, Skutt will lead them to the 
pond where Tuvstarr has disappeared.
SECOND SCENE – The Wild 
Forest
Read aloud to the Hunters: Sherazade remains silent for 
a moment, the audience motionless in ecstatic admiration. 
Then, the Weaver brings a glass of wine to her mouth, though 
she barely wets her lips. “You travel past the meadow covered 
with withered flowers, small villages, hills, and streams.  
The closer you get to the wilderness and the great forest, the 
more the atmosphere changes. The woods before you are 
shrouded in perpetual darkness. Only the faintest light of the 
sun hanging just beyond the horizon reaches this place. But the 
sun never really rises, leaving this land enclosed in a twilight 
and ghostly hour. The creatures of the woods have gone mad. 
Corrupted by the whispers in the grass, mesmerized by the 
moon’s reflections, they drink poisonous sap and foam that 
smells of sulphur. ”
Place: The wood that separates the kingdom from the 
deepest forest is basked in the faintest twilight glow. 
Time: Time has stopped and no longer has any meaning.
Information: When crossing the wood, the Hunters may

Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the 
Tuvstarr’s Reflection Scenario and can be acquired once 
the requirements are met. A Hunter can only have one 
Scenario Gift and if they acquire a new one, they will have 
to forgo the previous one. Unlike with the normal rules, in 
this case an Interlude it is not necessary to acquire the Gifts.
Hugin and Munin
Requirement: To meet Hugin and Munin and make a 
pact with them.
Once per Scene, you can ask for advice from Hugin and 
Munin. Ask the Storyteller a question, choosing from 
among: “Where should I go?” “How can I defeat that 
enemy?” “What is missing in my search?” The Storyteller 
will answer with a cryptic sentence, which always includes 
a useful hint. Get 1 Additional Success on all Defense 
Checks against an attack from a non-human creature, 
thanks to the blessing of Odin himself. At the beginning 
of each Scene, you must spend 1 Soma to feed the spirits of 
Hugin and Munin, or lose the use of this Gift for the rest of 
the Scenario.
Lance of the Fallen Knight
Requirement: Collect the lance of the knight who died in 
the search for Tuvstarr.
When you wield this spear and obtain an Outcome with 
Increment on an attack, you can inflict 1 Additional 
Wound. The tip of the lance can ignite at will, casting a light 
and allowing you to have a burning fire always near at hand.

lines: Permanently acquire a Scenario Gift

Experience in a 
Campaign game
During a Campaign, there are two types of Interludes that 
significantly alter the general rules.
Scenario Interludes
They are exactly like those presented on page 11, with the 
only exception that it is not possible to evolve a Descriptor 
into a Specialization.
Scenario’s end Interlude
These occur automatically at the end of each Scenario 
or after an Encounter if the Storyteller believes it was 
important to the Campaign. Each Hunter receives 3 XP 
for the end of the Scenario and 1 XP for an Encounter. 
They also recover all their Soma points and remove all the 
Wounds they suffered.
Y ou can spend 5 XP to get one of the following Advance-
ments.
 Permanently acquire a Scenario Gift
The Hunter gains the Gift earned during the Scenario or 
can purchase it now if the requirements have been met 
during the story. Using the Gift as a starting point, they will 
have to create a relevant Descriptor.
 Permanently acquire an Order’s 
Treasure
The Hunter gains an Order’s Treasure as a special 
equipment. They will have to create a relevant Descriptor 
for the Treasure.
 Acquire a new Gift
The Hunter gets a new Gift related to their story, which will 
be created with the rules on page 73. Together with the 
Gift, they will have to create a relevant Descriptor.
 Evolve a Descriptor into a Specialization
The Hunter can rewrite a Descriptor to make it more 
effective and powerful. Once per Scene, when they bring 
up the Descriptor in an action, they c

Durante un interludio se pueden gastar PX para obtener efec-
tos adicionales:
 ◆ Recuperar 2 puntos de Soma por PX.
 ◆ Curar 1 Herida por PX.
 ◆ Convertir un descriptor en una especialización. Durante 
el resto del escenario, la especialización otorga 1 Éxito adi-
cional la primera vez que se utiliza en cada escena (1 PX).
 ◆ Obtener un don de escenario hasta que este finalice (1 
PX).
Cada interludio, sin embargo, representa un uso del tiempo. 
Recuerda que el escenario, y los PNJs, pueden seguir actuando 
incluso cuando los Cazadores no están prestando atención.
Reglas para 
el Narrador
Broken Tales se ha concebido como un juego de rol modular 
y fácil de jugar. Para los Narradores que ya tienen experiencia 
con los juegos de rol, todas las reglas básicas se presentan en 
esta sección. En la sección Más detalles sobre las reglas del 
juego (página 17) se presentan todas las reglas explicadas con 
ejemplos para aclarar dudas del sistema y sobre cómo gestio-
nar una partida de Broken Tales.
Elige cómo jugar el escenario
A diferencia de otros juegos de rol, Broken Tales no tiene es-
cenarios con una trama ya escrita, sino que cultiva una narra-
tiva “emergente” . Lo que ocurra en el escenario será siempre 
el resultado de las acciones de los Cazadores y esto, combina-
do con las elecciones del Narrador respecto a los PNJs, gene-
rará situaciones con resultados impredecibles.
Una narrativa emergente significa acercarse al juego con la 
intención de dejarse sorprender, reacci

lines: Dones de escenario: si el Cazador ha cumplido ciertas
más de un don de escenario a la vez.
C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
101
Un árbol de escenas está formado por una serie de 
escenas vinculadas según esta estructura básica:
La primera escena lleva a la segunda, luego hay que elegir 
entre dos opciones para la tercera, la opción A y la opción 
B. La cuarta escena lleva a la quinta y luego habrá una nueva 
bifurcación o el epílogo, dependiendo de la duración del 
escenario. Cinco escenas con una bifurcación en el medio 
son óptimas para una sesión de unas tres horas. Las bifur -
caciones y escenas adicionales aumentan el tiempo de juego 
en aproximadamente treinta minutos cada una.
La primera escena debe estructurarse exactamente como he-
mos descrito, pero añadiendo un nuevo elemento: el final de 
la escena, que describe a grandes rasgos a qué nueva escena 
se trasladará la acción.
Por lo tanto, la nueva estructura de la escena es:
 ◆ Un lugar donde se desarrolla la escena.
 ◆ Un momento, para saber cuándo se produce en relación 
con la escena anterior.
 ◆ Una situación inicial que define lo que ocurre y lo que 
hay que describir a los Cazadores antes de empezar la 
Escena.
 ◆ Información que puede obtenerse en la escena. La in-
formación servirá de “guía” para las elecciones de los 
Cazadores, por lo que debe ser explícita. No tengas mie-
do de hacer que el juego esté demasiado encarrilado, 
¡eso es exactamente lo que buscamos!
 ◆ Final de la escena, que es una breve descripción de las 
consecuencias de la escena actual. El Narrador lo utili-
zará para dirigir las acciones de los PNJs y llamar

Durante una campaña, los Cazadores pueden 
utilizar un don de escenario adquirido previa-
mente en otro escenario si se ajusta al contexto 
y a los acontecimientos. Si esto ocurre, el ju-
gador debe poner esta posibilidad en conoci-
miento del Narrador, que decidirá (evaluando 
el impacto en el escenario actual) si permite 
al Cazador utilizar lo que ha aprendido en sus 
aventuras anteriores. Incluso en este caso, sin 
embargo, el límite sigue siendo 1 don de esce-
nario por Cazador.
Ejemplo: un don de escenario de Iskra de la caperucita roja.
Matalobos
Requisito: haberse enfrentado con los lobos en un combate al 
menos una vez.
Sabes cómo enfrentarte a los lobos más feroces y tienes cuidado 
de que no te rodeen. Cuando obtienes al menos un triunfo es­
tándar en una prueba de posición o defensa contra una manada 
de bestias, puedes eliminar inmediatamente a un animal PNJ 
menor con 1 Herida.
Don especial: un don reservado solo para el Cazador que es 
el villano del cuento original que inspiró el escenario que se 
está jugando, si está presente. Si cumple el requisito, el Cazador 
obtiene automáticamente el don durante el escenario, además 
de los que ya tenga y sin tener que gastar PX, ¡le guste o no! El 
don debe crearse con las reglas presentadas en el capítulo de 
creación de Cazadores. Ten en cuenta que el don debe represen-
tar una conexión entre el Cazador y el escenario, y no necesa-
riamente una mera ventaja. Al crear este don, no te preocupes 
demasiado por equil

lines: Dones de escenario
solo puede adquirir un don de escenario. Si adquieren
(don especial para Garou, el Viejo Lobo)
C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
127
Dones de escenario
Estos dones representan aptitudes especiales relacionadas 
con el escenario Iskra de la caperucita roja y pueden adqui-
rirse una vez se cumplan las condiciones. Cada Cazador 
solo puede adquirir un don de escenario. Si adquieren 
uno nuevo durante un interludio, tendrán que renunciar 
al anterior.
Voz de los espíritus
Requisito: haber estado en contacto con el don Espíritu.
Al calmar la mente y concentrarte, puedes abrirla al mun-
do espiritual y comunicarte con almas sin descanso. Gasta 
1 Soma para contactar con una criatura que tenga el don 
Espíritu y a la que hayas encontrado previamente. No sufres 
la Herida causada por el estrés de la conexión espiritual.
Matalobos
Requisito: haberse enfrentado con los lobos en un combate 
al menos una vez.
Sabes cómo enfrentarte a los lobos más feroces e intentas 
evitar que te rodeen. Cuando obtienes al menos un triunfo 
estándar en una prueba de posición o defensa contra una 
manada de bestias, puedes eliminar inmediatamente a 1 ani-
mal PNJ menor con 1 Herida.
Caperucita: ¿dónde vas tú tan solita?  
(don especial para Garou, el Viejo Lobo)
Requisito: encontrar a Iskra sola en el bosque.

C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:

Acontecimientos  
de escenario
Este escenario tiene seis acontecimientos. Como Narrador, 
no dudes en crear otros nuevos en respuesta a las acciones 
de los Cazadores.
Si los Cazadores presionan a Carlo: Lucino interven-
drá para ayudar a su amigo, dándole tiempo para escapar.
Si los Cazadores revelan su identidad a Vincenzo: el 
oficial estará a su disposición y tratará de ayudarles siempre 
pueda.
Si los Cazadores se encuentran con Geppetto:  el 
hombre intentará jugar con sus simpatías con la historia 
inventada del hijo perdido. Geppetto no es un genio, pero 
tampoco es tonto. Si descubre quiénes son realmente los 
Cazadores, intentará seguirles para ver si encuentran a 
Carlo.
Si los Cazadores se ganan la confianza de Carlo: el 
títere revelará la verdad. La culpa de todo la tienen Geppetto 
y Antonio. El títere ignora que ahora es Azzurra quien dirige 
las cosas.
Si Geppetto es capturado por los Cazadores: les con-
ducirá a Villa Cilesterino, donde se encuentra su compañero. 
Geppetto fingirá no saber nada, consciente de que cuando 
lleguen, Azzurra se deshará de los Cazadores.
Cuando Azzurra se encuentre con los Cazadores:  
intentará engañarlos haciéndose pasar por un espíritu bueno 
que vela por los habitantes de Sinalunga. Azzurra pedirá a 
los Cazadores que le ayuden a recuperar un títere mágico, 
animado por una fuerza oscura que está devorando las al -
mas de los pobres habitantes del pueblo.

Voz de los espíritus

o Espiritista.

1 Soma para contactar con una criatura que tenga el don 
Espíritu y a la que hayas encontrado previamente. Puedes 
comunicarte con el espíritu y entender lo que dice.
Exorcista
Requisito: tener un descriptor relacionado con la Iglesia 
o la magia. Hablar con Adrien o Alizée sobre las prácticas 
utilizadas contra los espíritus.
Has aprendido a utilizar tus habilidades contra los espíritus. 
Todas tus acciones pueden afectar a un objetivo que tenga el 
don Espíritu y todas las Heridas que inflijas no pueden ser 
ignoradas por ese don.

en las catacumbas:  Yvo

SEGUNDA ESCENA:  
El bosque salvaje
Lee lo siguiente a los Cazadores: Sherezade se queda en 
silencio durante un momento, con el público inmóvil en plena 
admiración. Entonces, la Tejedora se acerca un vaso de vino a 
la boca, aunque apenas se moja los labios, y dice:
—Cruzáis la pradera cubierta de flores marchitas y avanzáis 
a través de pequeñas aldeas, colinas y arroyos. Cuanto más os 
acercáis a las tierras salvajes y al gran bosque, más cambia el 
ambiente. El bosque que tenéis ante vosotros está envuelto en una 
oscuridad perpetua. Solo la más tenue luz del sol, que asoma por 
el horizonte, llega a este lugar. Pero el sol nunca sale realmente, 
dejando esta tierra encerrada en una hora crepuscular y fantas­
mal. Las criaturas del bosque han enloquecido y, corrompidas por 
los susurros de la hierba e hipnotizadas por los reflejos de la luna, 
beben savia venenosa y espuma con olor a azufre.
Lugar: la zona del bosque que separa el reino de sus profun-
didades, bañada por un tenue resplandor crepuscular.
Momento: el tiempo se ha

Dones de escenario
Estos dones representan aptitudes especiales relacionadas 
con el escenario El reflejo de Tuvstarr y pueden adquirirse 
una vez se cumplan os requisitos. Cada Cazador solo pue-
de adquirir un don de escenario. Si adquieren uno nuevo, 
tendrán que renunciar al anterior. A diferencia de las reglas 
habituales, en este caso no es necesario un interludio para 
adquirir los dones.
Hugin y Munin
Requisito: encontrar a Hugin y Munin y hacer un pacto 
con ellos.
Una vez por escena, puedes pedir consejo a Hugin y Munin. 
Formula una pregunta al Narrador, eligiendo entre: “ ¿A dón-
de debo ir?” , “ ¿Cómo puedo vencer a este enemigo?” o “ ¿Qué 
falta en mi búsqueda?” . El Narrador responderá con una fra-
se críptica, incluyendo siempre una pista útil. Además, obtie-
nes 1 Éxito adicional en todas las pruebas de defensa contra 
los ataques de criaturas no humanas gracias a la bendición 
del propio Odín. Al principio de cada escena, debes gastar 
1 Soma para alimentar a los espíritus de Hugin y Munin, o 
perderás el uso de este don durante el resto del escenario.
Lanza del caballero caído
Requisito: recuperar la lanza del caballero que murió en la 
búsqueda de Tuvstarr.
Cuando empuñas esta lanza y obtienes un triunfo con un 
incremento en un ataque, puedes infligir 1 Herida adicional. 
La punta de la lanza puede encenderse a voluntad, emitiendo 
luz y permitiéndote tener siempre un fuego ardiente a mano.

Using the Order’s Treasures
To the Hunters who cross the sacred threshold 
of Valea Viilor, remember this warning: In this 
place, priceless treasures are kept that must not 
be released upon the common world. God and 
his emissaries watch over the Treasury so that 
the power enclosed within it is used to fight the 
darkness.
Evil against Evil, the strength of the shadow 
turned against itself.
If you receive the honor of wielding one of the 
Treasures of the Order, remember the three 
rules by which you must abide:
1. Do not reveal the power of your treasure 
within view of the commons.
2. Do not use your treasure to harm a 
representative of the Church or the Order.
3. Do not abuse the power of the treasure 
and return it at the end of your task.
As a Hunter you are aware that one of your 
duties is to deliver any potential Treasure to the 
Order, so that it can be studied, cataloged, and 
made available to other brave Hunters in the 
fight against evil.
Obtaining and using an Order’s Treasure
The Order’s Treasury contains a series of iconic items that, 
like the Hunters and the Broken Tales, come from a wide 
variety of stories. Each Treasure has a story and grants 
those who wield it a Gift linked to its powers. There are 
several ways for a Hunter to use an Order’s Treasure:
Replace a Gift:  A Hunter chooses a Treasure that will 
replace one of their Gifts (they cannot replace the Dark 
Ego). They will then have the possibility to use the Treasure 
for the duration of

lines: the Seal of Disbelief Scenario Gift is necessary to activate it.

83
Threat – Seal of the Cauldron Fae
OL Medium 5
Descriptor: A magic seal capable of manifesting the magic 
of the Cauldron Fae.
Gift – Seals Everywhere:  At least one Seal of the 
Cauldron Fae is present in all the Places of the Scenario. 
Whoever possesses the Gang Seal or Personal Seal Gift  
can activate the effect of the Seal of the Cauldron Fae as an 
additional action; this allows them to teleport to another 
place featuring a Seal. The Seals are invisible to the human 
eye and can be used only by those who have a tattooed eye 
on their hand, placing said tattoo in front of the right eye. 
Those who use magic can detect the presence of a Seal, but 
the Seal of Disbelief Scenario Gift is necessary to activate it. 
Note: Some Places feature more than one Seal to grant 
the Lost Boys more narrative options in case the Hunters 
manage to impede access to a Seal or negate the Gift. 
George Carnarvon
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I am the head of the Carnarvon family and my 
service to the Crown has brought me a great deal of influence. 
I fear the occult and magic. I can’t sleep due to worry over my 
daughter’s abduction.
Agenda: Stop the gang of rebels that plagues London, but 
more than anything else find my beloved daughter Gwendolyn.
Carnarvon House
Descriptor: A rich and large mansion in the heart of 
London.
Joanna called Nana the Servant
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)
Descriptor: I have been Lady Gwendolyn’s trusted personal 
servant for over ten years

Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are six Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the Cauldron Fae reaches 4 Power Tokens:  she 
will be ready to kill all humans in London but will not do so 
until she has secured Pietrik safely in her lair.
If Pietrik understands the true objective of the 
Cauldron Fae: he will betray his mistress to try to save 
Gwendolyn and the Lost Boys.
If the Hunters free Gwendolyn: the young woman will 
tell the sad tale of Pietrik’s past, trying to make the Hunters 
understand that the boy acts out of resentment and not true 
evil.
If the Hunters capture one of the Lost Boys:  if put 
under pressure, he will reveal that the whole gang follows 
Pietrik’s lead and he is the only one who knows the identity 
of the sorceress who has bestowed powers on everyone. 
If the Hunters use Gwendolyn as bait for Pietrik:  
the young man will give up any involvement with the gang 
to be with his beloved. 
If the Lost Boys succeed in destroying the Skullem 
Factory: one of them will be injured in the explosion and 
can be captured by the Hunters.

These Gifts represent special abilities related to the I Don’t 
Believe Scenario and can be acquired once the conditions 
are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if 
they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous 
one.
Seal of Disbelief
Requirement: Capture or eliminate a member of the Lost 
Boys.

Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Hunger 
Island Scenario and can be acquired once the conditions are 
met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if they 
acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous one.
Stinky
Requirement: Meet Berend and learn from him how to 
mask your scent.
Y ou know how to make your scent unrecognizable and 
confuse enemies and predators. Y ou can spend 1 Soma and 
cover yourself with a rotting substance that will make you 
smelly. An enemy who bases their actions on smell (like a 
Maw) will never target you unless you act directly against 
them. Y ou gain 1 Additional Success to all Defense Checks 
you make to defend from an attack carried out by an enemy 
sensitive to your aroma. Y ou receive 1 Penalty at all Checks 
with ordinary people when your nauseating smell is a clear 
obstacle. 
The Stone that Came from the Sky
Requirement: Recover the stone that came from the sky 
from the slopes of the volcano.
Y ou hold an alien stone with great powers, which you can 
activate by suffering 1 Wound. When you do, choose from 
one of the following effects. 1. Might: Y our strength triples 
and you get 1 Additional Success to any Position Check 
based on sheer strength for the rest of the Scene. 2. Speed: 
Y our speed triples and you get 1 Additional Success at any 
Position Check based on speed for the rest of the Scene. 
3. Energy: Y our body is infused with pure energy and you 
receive 3 Energy Token

Scenario Events
In this Scenario, there are six Events. As a Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the actions of the 
Hunters.
If the Hunters pay too much attention to Abel:  
Svenn will intervene as soon as possible by making the 
young man disappear. Abel will be imprisoned in a pit in 
the hunting ground.
If the Hunters capture Bianca: Elsa and Karl will call 
a public assembly, trying to demonstrate to the Hunters 
how beloved Bianca is and that she does not deserve to be 
locked up by the Order.
If Bianca sees the people she cares about 
endangered by the Hunters: she will surrender of her 
own free will, trying to negotiate for the salvation of those 
she loves.
If the Hunters openly declare that they do not 
believe Bruna is guilty:  Karl and Elsa will make the 
Hunters understand that they are no longer welcome in 
Lohr and they will threaten to write a letter to the Prince 
to complain.
If the Hunters defeat or capture at least three 
Hessian mercenaries: the other mercenaries will try 
to start a negotiation with them. It will be clear that, for 
mercenaries, their comrades come first. 
The first night after the arrival of the Hunters: Elsa 
will go to the river with Svenn as her escort to meet Bianca 
and warn her of the presence of the Hunters.

Bloodwhite’s Heart Scenario and can be acquired once the 
conditions are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario 
Gift and if

Scenario Events
In this Scenario there are five Events. As a Storyteller, feel free 
to create new ones in response to the actions of the Hunters.
If the Hunters capture Bella:  the Lady of the House of 
Roses will threaten to unleash Dionysus’ wrath on all the 
innocents on the island. Bella will demand that the Hunters 
release her, while trying to negotiate a deal that works for 
everyone.
If the Hunters kill Dionysus:  Bella will thank them by 
telling them that it was all the fault of the mad nobleman 
who forced her to do everything. Her aim is to escape the 
judgment of the Church by passing herself off as a victim.
If the Hunters find Francesca:  the young woman will 
describe how all the Handmaids ignore what truly happens 
to Bella’s chosen ones. Francesca will tell the Hunters who 
Bellina really is, and how she brainwashed everyone on the 
island thanks to her position of power and the drugs in the 
food.
If the Hunters officially show up at the pier:  Bella 
will welcome them with all honors by offering them food 
and drinks. Bella’s purpose is to gather information about 
the newcomers and then feed them to Dionysus one by one.
If the Hunters accuse Bella or Dionysus:  Bella will 
seek an advantageous agreement by mentioning that the 
Gonsalva family is one of the greatest supporters of the 
Papacy, and that a scandal could end the huge subsidies that 
Pietro regularly pays to Rome.

Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the In your Eyes, 
with your Feet Scenario and can be acquired once the conditions 
are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if they 
acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous one.
Gift – Kind Blessing
Requirement: Show a sincere interest in solving the mysterious 
murders in Vigo to the Kind Mother.
A powerful witch has blessed you with protection against magic 
and sorcery. Receive 2 Additional Successes when you make a 
Defense Check against a magic effect. The first time you inflict 1 
Wound on a target using magic, the blessing dissipates until the 
next Interlude.
Note: The Storyteller can offer this Gift to the Hunter who has met 
the requirements without revealing who the witch who casts the 
blessing is. Simply, the Hunter will become aware that someone is 
trying to help them.

the Order)
Requirement: Defeat Francisca and Marta.

see page 25 of the Treasury of the Order.

lines: characteristics. A Coterie receives a special Gift for all of its
C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:
3
Introduction
With Lost Stories, the Broken Tales product line 
grows with new, never-before-seen content that expands 
the storytelling and campaigning options for both Story-
tellers and Players. In addition to a plethora of new Hunt-
ers, Dark Presences, and Treasures of Order, Lost Stories 
features several completely original pieces of content:
Nemesis
Five incredible enemies inspired by the heroic archetypes 
of classic fairytales. Nemesis introduces a new type of NPC 
and allows for the expansion of Scenarios and extension 
of the game’s Campaign, providing a set of adversaries as 
powerful as The One Who Plots in the Shadows.
Rome, City of the Order
The seat of the Papacy comes to life, showcasing locations, 
Encounters, and NPCs associated with the Order’s head-
quarters. Rome is a good starting point for any game Cam-
paign and a place to return to periodically to reorganize 
ideas for long-term play.
Coteries
Thematic rules for groups of Hunters who share similar 
characteristics. A Coterie receives a special Gift for all of its 
members, as well as special rules to address the issues and 
peculiarities of the Coterie itself.
The Villain’s Journey
A set of optional tables and rules to expand existing Scenar-
ios by adding all kinds of elements such as NPCs, locations, 
and more. The Villain’s Journey is also a useful tool for the 
Solo Sessions of the Untold Stories mode introduced in The 
Broken Ones. 
New Scenarios
As with every Broken Tales book, Lost Stories o

lines: Special Gift from a Scenario they have previously faced. To

62
Negative Attraction: The NPC is morbidly attracted to 
the Hunter because of some detail of one of the Hunter’s 
Descriptors, possibly a negative aspect that they want to 
encourage or draw out.
Positive Attraction: The NPC is attracted to a Hunter 
because of something positive in one of the Hunter’s De-
scriptors, and wants to understand it better.
Hatred: The NPC deeply hates the Hunter and wants to 
physically eliminate them or have them expelled from the 
Order.
Love: The NPC has feelings for the Hunter, a love that rep-
resents a bond they care about and want to develop. 
4. Consulting the Vatican Library
The Hunter spends entire days in the great Papal Library, 
searching for knowledge and information useful to their in-
vestigations. 
In this Scene, the Hunter has to decide what kind of re-
search to do.
Learning: Through ancient texts, the Hunter can learn a 
Special Gift from a Scenario they have previously faced. To 
obtain it, the Hunter must pay the appropriate XP . 
Research: The Hunter seeks information about anything 
strange in the world. The Storyteller secretly rolls a die and 
provides information on a specific topic.
1: Detailed information about another Hunter. 
2: Detailed information about a Treasure of the Order and 
its location, which will be the site of an unplayed Scenario. 
The Storyteller must incorporate the Treasure into the Sce-
nario by deciding who owns it or where it is located.

Scenario Gifts
These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Mother 
Heart Scenario and can be acquired once conditions are 
met. A Hunter can only acquire one Scenario Gift. If they 
buy a new one during an Interlude, they will have to forgo 
the previous one.
Exorcism
Requirement: To be taught the rites of exorcism 
by Father Neron Lobo. 
Y ou are able to sense damned souls, and this 
allows you to see and interact with all spiritu-
al beings. The Storyteller will alert you when 
a spirit is present, a feeling that manifests as a 
chill down your spine. Y ou can spend 1 Soma 
to open your mind to the spirits. If you do, you 
can communicate with them and perform ritu-
als and prayers to harm them for the rest of the 
Scene.
City Guide
Requirement: Succeed at least twice against 
the Threat - City Chaos.   
Y ou have figured out how to navigate the alleys 
of the chaotic city of Murcia. When you move 
from one Place to another, you automatically 
get a Standard Outcome on Threat Checks for 
City Chaos.

Scenario Events
There are six Events in this Scenario. As the Storyteller, feel 
free to create new ones in response to the Hunters’ actions.
If the Hunters have an NPC in their retinue: Nikola 
will arrive in Rovaniemi on the first evening to enslave the 
NPC. When threatened by the Hunter’s presence, he will 
flee by turning into mist. 
If the Hunters do not rescue the captive Hunter in 
the Hunting Lodge within one day:  Nikola will attack 
with his servants and take the Hunters to the toy factory to 
prepare them as a meal for the Badalisk.
If the Hunters capture Nikola:  The vampire will ask 
for their help in getting rid of the Badalisk, warning them 
that if the creature does not find a meal when it awakens, it 
will rampage through the village. 
If the Hunters eliminate the Badalisk before meet-
ing Nikola: The vampire will thank them and invite them 
to his mansion to understand the Order’s intentions toward 
him and to mediate a peaceful resolution. 
If the Hunters enter the mansion during the day:  Jori and Pete will attack the Hunters while Verna runs to 
wake her master. 
If the Hunters bring a Rovaniemi resident with 
them: Nikola will try to capture and enslave the resident, 
but under no circumstances will he risk their life.

are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift, and if 
they acquire a new one, they will hav

Demonic Dogs
OL Medium 5 - Main NPC  
(2 Wounds - 3 Dogs)
Descriptor: We are ancient demons and take the form of 
dogs to mingle with humans. We can fly and with our fangs, 
we tear at the soft flesh of our prey. 
Agenda: To kill as many humans as possible and thereby 
increase our power.     
Gift - Demonic Powers:  These creatures can fly, with 
all the narrative benefits that brings. As supernatural crea-
tures, Demonic Dogs are immune to all effects of fear and 
mind manipulation. Those who use magic against them 
take 1 Penalty to all related Checks and must pay 1 additio-
nal Soma if it is required to activate magic. A Demonic Dog 
can spend 1 Reset Token to increase its OL to Hard 7 for a 
single action, but not to defend itself. 
Information: When the Demonic Dogs are defeated, 
Britta or a witch who has read her diary can place a ma-
gical seal on the Magic Tinderbox: 
the object will lose some of 
its power and become the 
Treasure of the Order, 
The Tinderbox of Se-
cond Chances (see page 
28).

These Gifts represent special abilities related to The Last 
Wish Scenario and can be acquired once conditions are 
met. A Hunter can acquire one Scenario Gift. If they ac-
quire a new one during an Interlude, they will have to forgo 
the previous one.

By experiencing temporal shifts, you are able to notice the 
small differences in the fabric of reality. Once per Scene, 
you can spend 1 Som

lines: Scenario Gift - Fairy Crystal
C:/Users/Gamer/Documents/Broken Tales KS.sample:

Scenario Gift - Fairy Crystal
Requirement: Receive the Crystal as a gift from the Fair-
ies. (No need to spend XP)
Thanks to the power of the Crystal, you can see beyond il-
lusions and receive 3 Sight Tokens. Y ou can spend 1 Token 
and look through the Crystal to reveal an illusion, a dis-
guise, or the effect of magic that makes something appear 
different than it really is. When you spend the last Token, 
the Crystal shatters and you lose the effect of this Gift. 
 
Zone 10 – Circle of 
Stones
Description: Huge megalithic stones are arranged in a 
circle at the top of a low hill. The aura of ancient power is 
easily felt even by those unfamiliar with magic. 
Information: Inside the large stones are a series of small-
er stones that replicate the shape of the island. A person 
with a Descriptor related to magic can decipher the code 
of the stones and understand that the Circle is a system for 
magically moving from one place to another. Naming a 
Zone with the correct code will immediately transport ev-
erybody in the Circle there, but travel is only one-way and 
can’t be used to return to the Circle of Stones. Using the 
Fairy Crystal allows one to understand the workings of the 
Circle, even if the person looking at it does not understand 
magic.
Threat – Magic Menhirs
OL Hard 7
Descriptor: A circle of ancient stones laid at the dawn of 
time.

stones is easy, but getting exactly where you want to go is 
much les
